1: # DESIGN SYSTEM — DigitalBuho
2: 
3: > **Propósito:** Este documento describe **exactamente** lo que existe en el proyecto
4: > para que una IA de diseño (o cualquier desarrollador) pueda agregar o modificar
5: > pantallas sin inventar componentes, dependencias o patrones que no existen.
6: >
7: > **Idioma:** Este documento está en español porque el código fuente y los strings
8: > de la app están en español. Los nombres de clases, métodos, recursos y strings
9: > se escriben tal cual aparecen en el código.
10: 
11: ---
12: 
13: ## 1. IDENTIDAD DEL PROYECTO
14: 
15: | Atributo | Valor |
16: |----------|-------|
17: | Nombre | **Digital Buho** |
18: | Tipo | App Android nativa (asistencia con reconocimiento facial) |
19: | Plataforma | Android, minSdk 24, targetSdk 35, compileSdk 35 |
20: | Lenguaje | **Java 17** (NO Kotlin, NO Jetpack Compose) |
21: | UI | **ViewBinding** + XML layouts (NO Jetpack Compose, NO React, NO Vue) |
22: | Arquitectura | Clean-ish: `presentation/` → `domain/` → `data/` |
23: | DI | **Hilt 2.52** (`@HiltAndroidApp`, `@HiltViewModel`, `@AndroidEntryPoint`) |
24: | DB local | **Room 2.6.1** |
25: | Red | **Retrofit 2.11.0** + **OkHttp 4.12.0** |
26: | Backend API | `http://[REDACTED:ipv4]:8000/api/v1/` (cambiable en `app/build.gradle:23`) |
27: | Background | **WorkManager 2.10.0** (sync offline) |
28: 
29: ---
30: 
31: ## 2. TECH STACK
32: 
33: ### Dependencias principales (desde `gradle/libs.versions.toml`)
34: 
35: | Categoría | Librería | Versión |
36: |-----------|----------|---------|
37: | Core | `androidx.appcompat:appcompat` | 1.7.0 |
38: | Core | `androidx.core:core` | 1.15.0 |
39: | Core | `androidx.activity:activity` | 1.9.3 |
40: | Core | `androidx.fragment:fragment` | 1.8.5 |
41: | Core | `androidx.constraintlayout:constraintlayout` | 2.2.1 |
42: | Lifecycle | `androidx.lifecycle:lifecycle-viewmodel` | 2.8.7 |
43: | Lifecycle | `androidx.lifecycle:lifecycle-livedata` | 2.8.7 |
44: | Lifecycle | `androidx.lifecycle:lifecycle-runtime` | 2.8.7 |
45: | Material | `com.google.android.material:material` | 1.12.0 |
46: | Cámara | `androidx.camera:camera-core` | 1.4.1 |
47: | Cámara | `androidx.camera:camera-camera2` | 1.4.1 |
48: | Cámara | `androidx.camera:camera-lifecycle` | 1.4.1 |
49: | Cámara | `androidx.camera:camera-view` | 1.4.1 |
50: | ML | `com.google.mlkit:face-detection` | 16.1.7 (standalone, sin Firebase) |
51: | ML | `com.google.ai.edge.litert:litert` | 1.0.1 (TFLite) |

53: | Room | `androidx.room:room-runtime` | 2.6.1 |
54: | Room | `androidx.room:room-compiler` (KAPT) | 2.6.1 |
55: | Retrofit | `com.squareup.retrofit2:retrofit` | 2.11.0 |
56: | Retrofit | `com.squareup.retrofit2:converter-gson` | 2.11.0 |
57: | OkHttp | `com.squareup.okhttp3:okhttp` | 4.12.0 |
58: | OkHttp | `com.squareup.okhttp3:logging-interceptor` | 4.12.0 |
59: | WorkManager | `androidx.work:work-runtime` | 2.10.0 |
60: | Hilt | `com.google.dagger:hilt-android` | 2.52 |

64: | Glide | `com.github.bumptech.glide:glide` | 4.16.0 |
65: | Gson | `com.google.code.gson:gson` | 2.11.0 |
66: | Test | `junit:junit` | 4.13.2 |
67: | Test | `org.mockito:mockito-core` | 5.14.2 |
68: | Test | `androidx.test.ext:junit` | 1.2.1 |
69: | Test | `androidx.test.espresso:espresso-core` | 3.6.1 |

71: ### Comandos de build
72: 
73: ```bash
74: ./gradlew assembleDebug          # build APK debug
75: ./gradlew test                   # tests unitarios (JVM)
76: ./gradlew connectedAndroidTest   # tests instrumentados (dispositivo)
77: ./gradlew lint                   # Android lint
78: ```
79: 
80: ---
81: 
82: ## 3. PALETA DE COLORES

1643:   function handleHash() {
1644:     const hash = window.location.hash.replace('#', '');
1645:     if (hash && screenMap[hash] && hash !== currentScreen) {
1646:       if (currentScreen === 'login' && hash !== 'login') {
1647:         navigateTo(hash, 'forward');
1648:       } else if (currentScreen !== 'login') {
1649:         const navOrder = ['home', 'schedule', 'profile'];
1650:         const fromIdx = navOrder.indexOf(currentScreen);
1651:         const toIdx = navOrder.indexOf(hash);
1652:         if (fromIdx !== -1 && toIdx !== -1) {
1653:           navigateTo(hash, toIdx > fromIdx ? 'forward' : 'back');
1654:         } else {
1655:           navigateTo(hash, 'forward');
1656:         }
1657:       }
1658:     }
1659:   }

1664:   function applyTheme(theme) {
1665:     document.documentElement.classList.add('theme-changing');
1666:     document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', theme);
1667:     requestAnimationFrame(() => {

<skill_content name="ui-ux-pro-max">
# Skill: ui-ux-pro-max

# UI/UX Pro Max - Design Intelligence

Comprehensive design guide for web and mobile applications. Contains 50+ styles, 161 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 161 product types with reasoning rules, 99 UX guidelines, and 25 chart types across 10 technology stacks. Searchable database with priority-based recommendations.

## When to Apply

This Skill should be used when the task involves **UI structure, visual design decisions, interaction patterns, or user experience quality control**.

### Must Use

This Skill must be invoked in the following situations:

- Designing new pages (Landing Page, Dashboard, Admin, SaaS, Mobile App)
- Creating or refactoring UI components (buttons, modals, forms, tables, charts, etc.)
- Choosing color schemes, typography systems, spacing standards, or layout systems
- Reviewing UI code for user experience, accessibility, or visual consistency
- Implementing navigation structures, animations, or responsive behavior
- Making product-level design decisions (style, information hierarchy, brand expression)
- Improving perceived quality, clarity, or usability of interfaces

### Recommended

This Skill is recommended in the following situations:

- UI looks "not professional enough" but the reason is unclear
- Receiving feedback on usability or experience
- Pre-launch UI quality optimization
- Aligning cross-platform design (Web / iOS / Android)
- Building design systems or reusable component libraries

### Skip

This Skill is not needed in the following situations:

- Pure backend logic development
- Only involving API or database design
- Performance optimization unrelated to the interface
- Infrastructure or DevOps work
- Non-visual scripts or automation tasks

**Decision criteria**: If the task will change how a feature **looks, feels, moves, or is interacted with**, this Skill should be used.

## Rule Categories by Priority

*For human/AI reference: follow priority 1→10 to decide which rule category to focus on first; use `--domain <Domain>` to query details when needed. Scripts do not read this table.*

| Priority | Category | Impact | Domain | Key Checks (Must Have) | Anti-Patterns (Avoid) |
|----------|----------|--------|--------|------------------------|------------------------|
| 1 | Accessibility | CRITICAL | `ux` | Contrast 4.5:1, Alt text, Keyboard nav, Aria-labels | Removing focus rings, Icon-only buttons without labels |
| 2 | Touch & Interaction | CRITICAL | `ux` | Min size 44×44px, 8px+ spacing, Loading feedback | Reliance on hover only, Instant state changes (0ms) |
| 3 | Performance | HIGH | `ux` | WebP/AVIF, Lazy loading, Reserve space (CLS < 0.1) | Layout thrashing, Cumulative Layout Shift |
| 4 | Style Selection | HIGH | `style`, `product` | Match product type, Consistency, SVG icons (no emoji) | Mixing flat & skeuomorphic randomly, Emoji as icons |
| 5 | Layout & Responsive | HIGH | `ux` | Mobile-first breakpoints, Viewport meta, No horizontal scroll | Horizontal scroll, Fixed px container widths, Disable zoom |
| 6 | Typography & Color | MEDIUM | `typography`, `color` | Base 16px, Line-height 1.5, Semantic color tokens | Text < 12px body, Gray-on-gray, Raw hex in components |
| 7 | Animation | MEDIUM | `ux` | Duration 150–300ms, Motion conveys meaning, Spatial continuity | Decorative-only animation, Animating width/height, No reduced-motion |
| 8 | Forms & Feedback | MEDIUM | `ux` | Visible labels, Error near field, Helper text, Progressive disclosure | Placeholder-only label, Errors only at top, Overwhelm upfront |
| 9 | Navigation Patterns | HIGH | `ux` | Predictable back, Bottom nav ≤5, Deep linking | Overloaded nav, Broken back behavior, No deep links |
| 10 | Charts & Data | LOW | `chart` | Legends, Tooltips, Accessible colors | Relying on color alone to convey meaning |

## Quick Reference

### 1. Accessibility (CRITICAL)

- `color-contrast` - Minimum 4.5:1 ratio for normal text (large text 3:1); Material Design
- `focus-states` - Vis

content:
Listo. `app.html` fue reescrito completamente basado en el `DESIGN_SYSTEM.md` real de Digital Buho + los principios de `ui-ux-pro-max`:

**Cambios principales:**

| Categoría | Antes | Ahora |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| **Paleta** | Accent `#1E2B8F`, bg `#f8f9ff` | Accent `#1E2D8F`, bg `#F0F2F8` (exactos del XML real) |
| **Tipografía** | Hanken Grotesk (custom) | Roboto / system sans-serif (como la app real) |
| **Dimensiones** | Variables inconsistentes | 100px logo, 20px márgenes, 56px botones, 12px radius (igual al XML) |
| **Pantalla nueva** | No existía | **Confirmación** — "¡Asistencia Registrada!", detalles, sincronización, empresa |
| **Flujo completo** | Login → Home independientes | **"Marcar Asistencia"** → loading → **Confirmación** → Volver al Inicio |
| **Strings** | Mezcla inglés/español | 100% español del `strings.xml` real |
| **Accesibilidad** | Sin aria-labels | `aria-label`, `aria-current="page"`, `aria-selected`, `aria-live`, `role="tablist"`, focus rings |
| **Reduced motion** | No soportado | `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` en media query |
| **Contraste** | No verificado | `#1C1C2E` sobre `#F0F2F8` = ~10:1 en ambos temas |
| **Dark mode** | Tokens incompletos | Overrides para cada token (surface-gray, danger-bg-alt, badges, compliance card) |
| **SVG logo** | Ícono genérico | Edificio con documento (match al `ic_logo_building` real) |
